title: fix: deactivated community models return generic 400, not a special 500
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Deactivated community models now behave like any unknown model — plain `400 Invalid model or alias`, no reason exposed to API callers
- Removes `resolveIncludingDisabled` and the special 500 error path added in #12150
- Owner-facing reason display (My Models dashboard) is unaffected — untouched in `enter.pollinations.ai`

## Test plan
- [x] `gen.pollinations.ai` full suite: 438/438 passing
- [x] `community-endpoints.test.ts` updated: deactivated model call → 400, message does not contain the disabled reason
- [x] typecheck + biome clean

gist.headline: 400 is the new 500
gist.blurb: Calling a deactivated community model now returns a standard 400 error instead of a messy 500. A cleaner, more predictable API experience for your apps.
gist.summary: Deactivated community models now return a standard 400 'Invalid model or alias' error instead of a 500 error, hiding the specific deactivation reason from API callers.
gist.impact: API consumers will now receive predictable 400 errors when requesting deactivated models, while model owners can still see the specific deactivation reasons in their dashboard.
gist.visual_concept: The Nomnom creature eating a messy 500 error log while the bee mascot holds up a clean, neat 400 sign, representing the cleanup of API error codes.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating a messy red '500' error document, while the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt holds up a clean, neat '400' sign. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like A Short Hike. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
